message:
Add custom illustration and architecture diagram as webp assets

Add two project-specific images generated for the README:

- frankensqlite_illustration.webp (215 KB, 1536×1024): A stylized
  Frankenstein monster at a workshop bench assembling a database engine
  from pages, WAL frames, and MVCC version chains. Visual references to
  key project concepts: compatibility mode / native mode toggle, RaptorQ
  repair symbols, page-level locking, SSI serializable snapshots, and
  the #[forbid(unsafe_code)] policy.

- frankensqlite_diagram.webp (238 KB, 1024×1536): The 23-crate layered
  architecture rendered as a Frankenstein-themed vertical flowchart.
  Shows the full dependency hierarchy: fsqlite-cli → fsqlite → fsqlite-core
  branching into SQL Layer (parser, AST, planner, VDBE, func), Storage
  Layer (btree, pager, WAL, MVCC, VFS), and Extensions (fts3, fts5,
  rtree, json, session, icu, misc), all converging on the foundation
  crates fsqlite-types and fsqlite-error.

Both converted from source PNGs using cwebp (illustration at q80,
diagram at q60) to hit the ~250 KB target. Source PNGs are excluded
via a new *.png rule in .gitignore since only the optimized webp
versions need to be tracked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
